rulings.Case Title: JOHN A LYNCH VS. THOMAS S WU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Related Shareholder Derivative Actions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 19, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFF JOHN LYNCH'S Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Related Shareholder Derivative Actions Denied, without prejudice. The moving party failed to show whether UCBH's bankruptcy has been discharged to release the automatic stay in order for the court to hear this motion. Further, counsel's declaration regarding the bankruptcy court proceedings (see, e.g., Bottini Decl., 5) is insufficient without copies of the relevant court orders. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 19, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT SUTTER HEALTH MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment is granted. rulings.Case Title: RIMMA BOROKHOV et al VS. FREEDOM WEST HOMES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena And For A Protective Order And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 19, 2013, Line 1. PLAINTIFFS RIMMA BOROKHOV, DAVID TSE, AND MICHAEL TO'S Motion To Quash Subpoena And For A Protective Order And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part. The subpoena duces tecum dated February 12, 2013, served on Wells Fargo on February 22, 2013, is modified as follows: The scope of examination or inquiry is limited to the following topics - (1) plaintiff Rimma Borokhov's compensation at the time of her termination from employment with Wells Fargo (and supporting records), (2) the reason(s) for Ms. Borokhov's termination from employment with Wells Fargo (and supporting documents). The parties' requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CASEY SECURITIES LLC VS. RICHARD CASEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Stay Enforcement Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 19, 2013, Line 8, RESPONDENTS RICHARD CASEY, GLORIA CASEY'S (Amended) Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Stay Enforcement Of Judgment Denied without prejudice. Richard Casey and Gloria Casey, as co-Trustees of the Casey Family Trust, have not demonstrated good cause to stay enforcement of the judgment in this action during the pendency of Casey, et al. v. Casey Securities, LLC, et al. (case no. CGC-13-528764). The evidence proffered in support of the motion is insufficient to support the court exercising its discretion on any of the three factors enumerated in CCP § 918.5(b). Casey Securities, LLC's opposition exceeded the 15 page limit without seeking leave of Court. CRC 31113(d) and (e)). The Court did not read beyond page 15. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RICHARD TOM VS. JEREMY D BLANK et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 19, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT JEREMY BLANK'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action. Plaintiff has not pled-as he must--that he was actually innocent of the underlying crime and did not oppose the demurrer as it related to this cause of action. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as it relates to the second, third, fourth, and fifth causes of action. Plaintiff is granted 10 days leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege breach of contract, breach of fiduciary duties, breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing, and/or fraud/negligent misrepresentation that do not implicate the quality of the representation provided in the underlying litigation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: OMAR IKFAFI VS. ZAKOUT, NASER D/B/A THEATER TOO CAFE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 19, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT NASER ZAKOUT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained, with 10 day leave to amend, Mr. Zakout's demurrer to the fourth cause of action for promissory estoppel, and overruled as to the remaining causes of action. For the fourth cause of action, promissory estoppel pertains to a binding promise made without consideration. (Fontenot v. Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 256, 275.) In this case, Mr. Ikfafi alleges that that a written agreement was entered into where consideration was bargained for, and thus the doctrine of promissory estoppel is inapplicable. Leave is granted if Mr. Ikfafi can plead in good faith a promise made by Mr. Zakout without consideration. The remaining causes of action are adequately pled. Whether Mr. Ikfafi was a partner or employee is a question of fact that is not suitable on a demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CASEY FAMILY TRUST et al VS. CASEY SECURITIES , LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Attendance At Deposition And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 19, 2013, Line 3. PLAINTIFFS CASEY FAMILY TRUST, RICHARD CASEY, AND GLORIA CASEY Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition And Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No tentative; parties to appear. However, parties are ordered in advance of the hearing on April 19, 2013 to meet and confer in an effort to reach agreement between themselves as to the status of discovery proceedings pending defendants' appeal of the order denying defendants' motion to compel arbitration. If the parties are able to reach agreement and moot the motion, the parties should notify the Court and the pro tem (katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com). If the parties are not able to reach agreement and this matter remains for hearing, the parties will be expected to report at the hearing on their meet-and-confer progress. =(302/JPT)
